text.title: Distribution Build and Founder-Plan Overlay
text.summary: How to produce P25/P50/P75 distributions per archetype and overlay the founder's plan with a green/yellow/red flag system.

rule.statement: Quote distribution (P25/P50/P75), never a single median. Single medians create false precision and hide the strategic range the founder needs to understand.
rule.rationale: The spread between P25 and P75 is the actionable signal. A P25-P75 ARPU spread of $15-$80 in a category tells the founder the model has not standardized. A $40-$50 spread tells them it has.

section.title: Distribution Build

list.item: Compute P25/P50/P75 per archetype AND per stage (seed/Series-B/listed)
Flag comps more than 2 standard deviations from the median with a footnote explaining the cause (e.g., compute COGS, hardware revenue, geographic discount)
Annotate trends: last 4-8 quarters delta per metric (e.g., usage-based share moved from 20% to 38% of new ARR in dev-tools cohort 2022-2025)
Flag any P50 metric that shifts more than 10% quarter-over-quarter as a "regime change" warrant for the trend-analysis methodology

section.reference: Script emitting P25/P50/P75 per archetype from comps.csv

list.item: Green: founder's planned metric within P25-P75 for their archetype and stage
Yellow: founder's metric within P10-P25 or P75-P90 — flagged as ambitious or conservative
Red: founder's metric outside P10-P90 — requires explicit justification or model revision

rule.statement: Do not rewrite the founder's plan. Emit a delta only — which cells are out-of-band and what is the most likely source of the discrepancy (model choice, stage mismatch, geography, or pricing page vs. realized ACV).
rule.rationale: Rewriting the plan conflates the market-researcher deliverable (distributions) with the product-manager deliverable (decisions). The researcher surfaces the gap; the founder owns the response.

rule.statement: Re-run the benchmark every 2 quarters. Flag any P50 metric that shifts more than 10% as a "regime change" and open an SDD task for the product-manager to revise the roadmap ARR targets.
rule.rationale: SaaS economics shift materially over 6-month periods in fast-moving categories. A benchmark that is 12 months old guides product and pricing decisions toward the wrong target distribution.
